title: Kate Hudson: Song Sung Blue Star Returns to the Oscars Race
text: Kate Hudson in 'Song Sung Blue' - Key Information. Role & Performance: Kate Hudson plays Claire Sardina, one half of a Neil Diamond tribute duo called 'Lightning and Thunder.' Her character undergoes a dramatic transformation midway through the film when she's hit by a car, shifting from playful to somber as she grapples with shattered dreams. Awards Recognition: Hudson has received major nominations for the role: Golden Globes: Best Actress in a Musical/Comedy; Actor Awards (formerly SAG Awards): Lead Actress nomination. This marks her first Oscar-contending role in 25 years since her breakout in 'Almost Famous.' Production Insights: Hudson stated: 'There was a real risk — for me it was like, this could either be really great, or go really wrong.' The film was rejected by over a dozen studios before Focus Features acquired it. Box Office Success: The film has emerged as a theatrical sleeper hit, dropping less than 20% in its second weekend and grossing over $30 million worldwide. It maintains a 97% verified Rotten Tomatoes audience score—20 points above critics' ratings. Personal Reflections: Hudson emphasized the experience as deeply fulfilling: 'It's like, 'Me time!' I'm doing this for myself,' regarding the awards campaign process.
